title: Podman build fails if can't find any files on glob match for COPY statement
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman build` fails on `COPY` or `ADD` when some the 2nd argument of copy is glob match and doesn't exist:

It is desireable to do not fail if glob match doesn't return files because it makes possible to add some files optionally. See https://stackoverflow.com/a/46801962/1380712

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
COPY this-path-exists this-glob-match-doesnt-exist* .
```

**Describe the results you received:**

This Dockerfile fails with `podman build .`

**Describe the results you expected:**

This Dockerfile passes with `docker build .`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

OS: Ubuntu 20.04.2 LTS on Windows 10 x86_64 (WSL2)

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out. I'm moving this issue over to Buildah.

This is fixed in current podman 3.2 release.
Buildah 1.21.0
